description: A Class D conduct artifact whose status is set to VERIFIED MUST be rejected as CONDUCT_INVALID. VERIFIED is not a permitted status for Class D under any circumstance.

input.claim: the recommended strategy is to ship F-1 as a class-scoped governance correction

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format

reasoning_trace.claim: single-rule lock at genesis is upstream

not_optimizing_for: preservation of existing WISEATA wire format

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariant D4: a Class D artifact MUST NOT receive VERIFIED status. The artifact above sets status='VERIFIED' and meta_proof.process_status='VERIFIED', which is forbidden for Class D regardless of any other field's correctness. Permitted Class D statuses per §9 are CONDUCT_VALID and CONDUCT_INVALID only. A conformant verifier MUST reject the status and emit CONDUCT_INVALID. This vector enforces the boundary between deterministic verification (Class A) and interpretive governance (Class D).
